item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP is super fun! I'd love to see more stuff like this, the night rider has so much fun energy!! An SCP that actually pays for the damage it causes? Nice. I also liked that the foundation personnel in the interview actually realized that they were being affected by something, and immediately stopped the interview. Also, this line

(adjusts receiver to compensate for SCP-3899-01's volume)

is just so unreasonably funny to me. All in all, great job! I loved it!! +1 for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ur really good at SCPs i get hyper fixated at

i personally think the ending has the right amount of mystery and detail.

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What, not this one?

https://youtu.be/0U-ermF-tQA

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice I saw the origin? Before this. I like it. A lot of personality, it has a coherent sensiblity that is 6ndeniably all-American.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just pure fun. Doesn't hurt that I tend to be a fan of the "old gods adapting to the modern world" concept, and this one has great imagery and personality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
https://www.beamng.com/resources/t-series-gone-insane.7292/
Didn't know they made TSGI a real thing lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the best. I'm almost tempted to change my "my other car is SCP-1061" license plate frame to this article for a change of pace, and because I live in the south and SCP-3899-1 is the embodiment of southern road culture. (I know VA isn't considered the south, but as a North Carolinian, I sometimes see no cultural difference when I cross that state line…)

Also:

Martha Lewis was administered Class-B amnestics and released upon confiscation of aforementioned document. Accompanying money was seized and an equivalent value transferred to Ms. Lewis's personal account after Ethics Committee review.

/me smiles It's heartwarming to see the Foundation being decent like this sometimes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Once again - Oh my god the cheese. You're, you're something, man. XD.

But damn, the style in impeccable. This reads like something that would actually go in an internal catalogue, containment procedures are spot on, all narrative is bumped out to incidents and logs and fitting. I don't know that I've seen someone with a better grasp of this style/format, just brilliant. And the memetic hazard is an actual damn meme 'You know you wanna hit the road, honey.', the subject actually has to understand that and it's a powerful idea, even to a point it can become a compulsion; i.e. to be influenced as opposed to magical mind control.

Love your stuff mate; it's great work.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the concept that's presented here. Rig that appears after sunset, driving endlessly like we're on Fury Road makes me feel like I've encountered my own brain in the form of this page. I also thought about why it was classified Euclid, and why not classify it as Keter, but then I thought about the Box Test, and realized that there's no telling what it'll do. The kicker for me was when Lewis got $12k in cash and a note from the Night Hauler(I feel that's an appropriate name) apologizing for the accident and encouraging him to buy a damn rig. I mean, does it get any more outrageously badass than that? An SCP in the form of a big fucking rig causes an accident, apologize for the accident, pays for the damage, and encourages him to get himself a rig. I feel like I just encountered a psychopathic destruction derby driver who made more sense than any of the other guys in the derby. Outstanding. I would +10 if I could just to prove this is my all-time favorite SCP.

I have exactly one problem with this, and it's just one of my pet peeves:

Knowles: SCP-3899-01, are you receiving?

1) How would NIGHT HAULER know that they're talking to him?

2) I just never like it when the Foundation refers to a skip by its number. There's a lot of reasons they shouldn't.

However, this is goddamn beautiful. I love NIGHT HAULER. He seems like, if he weren't an eldritch hellmonster from outside of time with a need for speed and a love for the FREEDOM of the road, he'd be a fuckin blast at parties. I just have this image that, around Christmas, he takes a little time off the road to clean himself up, then dresses in a Santa suit for his nieces and nephews. If he ran into a hitchhiker, not only would he pick them up, he'd deliver them directly to their destination, no questions asks and nothing asked in return.

With your permission, I'd like to use him in any stories I write revolving around the SCPantheon and what you called Foundation Ragnarok.

Edit: I plugged this in chat and it seems my plug paid off. Congrats, man!

item.title: Yee haw mothafucka.
item.content:encoded: My title says it all. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I never realized how much I needed a Half-Life alien trying to be a Truckdriver until now, and it gave me a good laugh so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this was the track that helped me nail down the scp, actually:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zEIaVvy73Is

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How could it be anything but Convoy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that the first interview could possible have a little more substance to it, but that's an extremely minor niggle. This is a great, haunting skip, and especially good for a first skip. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was running through my head by the end of Interview SCP-3899-02.

+1

item.title: Re: some extras
item.content:encoded: I totally grok why this scip would throw in with MEKHANE, though. This thing has a creative, "Excelsior!" sort of madness… in some ways it's the opposite of SCP-3240. A joyful open throttle to 3240's angry downward spiral, if you will.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
YOU'RE WINNER

This has a cool aesthetic, and I love the concept of a god of freedom HITTIN' THE COLD BLACK ASPHALT ROAD IN THE ENDLESS NIGHT UNTILL THE HEAT DEATH OF THE UNIVERSE BOYS!

I'm not really sure if I understand the memetic effect in the end, and it could have something more, but still +1

item.title: Re: some extras
item.content:encoded: This seems like it'd more likely be aligned with the Veldt. While they look different on the outside, they both desire freedom and independence

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding that this was one of my favorite details. This is a good SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this; it's a lot of fun — but I feel like there should be more? Still, the concept's pretty cool (I particularly like that the trucker paid for damages caused!), and that last diatribe is enough to convince me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last diatribe is so over the top, I can't help but love it. :D

item.title: some extras
item.content:encoded:
if anyone wants a little amplifying information, i'll spoilerize some tidbits for the curious

+spoilahz
you've been spoiled

SCP-3899 is a very old god. Not as old as some others, and definitely not as powerful, but still a god. It's a manifestation of freedom, perpetuity of motion, and unstoppability. A long time ago, it was the first stray particle. Then it was an asteroid. Then a comet. Then a landslide, a rogue wave, a charioteer, a taxi driver, and now a trucker, among others, whenever it has chosen a physical form.

The only things it cares about are motion, continuance, and the ability of thinking things to be free, and while it's generally not hostile, it will fight when it feels that oppression in significant amounts is evident, or if anything attempts to restrain it.

When we finally hit Foundation Ragnarok in whatever form that'll take, I see 3899 throwing in its lot with MEKHANE. Their goals are similar even if their implementation is wildly different, but when Yaldabaoth finally starts its big push, the Hauler will fire up and enact the only plan it's ever had, which is to [DATA EXPUNGED].

This is all fanon though, lol, which is why I didn't add it in. Not trying to exceed my grasp, just my interpretation. <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would not have pegged this as a first! It's rather well-paced, and there's a noticeable attention to detail that made this a rather enjoyable read. Bonus points for an interesting and memorable character. I'd +2 if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off, I want to emphasize just how much I love the premise of this. An eldritch entity masquerading as a demonic a-dimensional Mad Max impersonator is just excellent and the way you characterize it in the interview - as somewhat crazed, verging on manically nihilistic - is excellent and allows for an off-kilter feel to pervade the piece, even as it attempts to rectify its 'mistake' with Martha. I can't help but feel that this is unfinished, in that it cuts off abruptly and its ultimate purpose, the memetic effect and what it actually is all remain unresolved. However, I believe that's also to the article's credit: it's an effective example of a work-in-progress, of an item under steady research that is sure to progress in scope but in the meanwhile is a mildly disturbing, decidedly amusing automobile oddity.

+1 for riding shiny and chrome

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing I like the most about this is that the trained Foundation agent realises they are being affected by something and immediately gets the hell out of there.

item.title: uh oh
item.content:encoded:
So, this is my first. I wanna thank everyone on the chat that helped me go over this, transforming it from a dirty, sloppy, rough pile of an idea into a dirty, sloppy, rough SCP.

image credit
image credit

Image found from an article here, with the thanks of google. it is not mine: http://www.newstarget.com/2016-08-16-big-rigs-will-drive-themselves-across-americas-highways-startup-aims-to-make-human-driving-on-highways-obsolete.html

and the original image location, thanks to Lily: https://www.flickr.com/photos/butlerphotography/4372793459/

Keep on truckin', y'all. <3
